sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Mallorca, currently sitting at 11th in the league with 44 points, host Real Valladolid, who are struggling at the bottom of the table in 20th place with just 16 points. This matchup at Estadi de Son Moix in Palma presents a significant opportunity for Mallorca to capitalize on their higher standing. Real Valladolid’s dire position makes this a must-win game for them to avoid further relegation trouble. The referee for this clash, Juan Luis Pulido Santana, averages 4.8 yellow cards per game, indicating a potentially feisty encounter. With a red card average of 0.27 per game, there’s a slight chance of a sending-off as well.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, results have been evenly split with 2 wins each for Mallorca and Real Valladolid, alongside 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on September 27, 2024, saw Mallorca triumph 2-1 away at Real Valladolid, with Pablo Maffeo earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.7. In head-to-head trends, Mallorca have gone 3 games without a loss against Valladolid, while over 2.5 goals have been scored in 8 of their last 10 meetings. Additionally, both teams have seen over 4.5 cards in their last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting a physical battle ahead.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Mallorca are currently on a worrying streak of 3 games without a win, failing to score in those matches as well. However, their home games have often been low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 home outings. On the other hand, Real Valladolid are in a dreadful run of form, with 8 consecutive losses and 15 games without a win overall. They’ve also failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 15 matches, while their games often see over 2.5 goals, as evidenced in 6 of their last 6 away fixtures. Both teams have shown a tendency for fewer than 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 8 combined matches.

sections.subtitle: Mallorca’s Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Mallorca will miss key forward Vedat Muriqi, who has scored 7 goals this season with an expected goals value of 9.62, alongside Takuma Asano, who has 2 goals. Despite these absences, players like Dani Rodríguez, with 4 goals and 7 assists, and Sergi Darder, with 5 assists, will be crucial in midfield. Defender Antonio Raíllo, boasting a strong average Sofascore rating of 7.13, will anchor the defense. Additionally, Pablo Maffeo, with a rating of 7.0, could replicate his heroics from the last meeting against Valladolid.

sections.subtitle: Real Valladolid’s Key Players and Challenges
sections.text: Real Valladolid’s attacking hopes rest on Raúl Moro, who has 4 goals and 4 assists this season, and Iván Sánchez, a standout in past matchups with an average Sofascore rating of 7.26 and 2 goals against Mallorca in the last 5 games. However, they’ll be without forward Mamadou Sylla, who has contributed 5 goals this season. Defensively, Adam Aznou stands out with a league rank of 4 for aerial duels won at 77.8%, while Darwin Machís also ranks high at 5 with a 75% aerial duel win rate. These strengths could test Mallorca’s aerial game, where they rank 6th in the league with 52.48% aerial duels won.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Mallorca have struggled offensively, ranking 18th in the league with just 31 goals scored, though their defense is relatively solid, conceding 39 goals and ranking 7th for least goals conceded. They excel in accurate crosses, leading the league with 196, and rank 3rd in clearances per game at 27.7. Real Valladolid’s defensive woes are evident, with a streak of 15 games without a clean sheet and conceding first in 8 of their last 10 matches. Mallorca’s goal distribution shows a peak in the 46-60 minute period with 9 goals, representing 20.5% of their total, while Valladolid often strike late, with 27.7% of their goals coming between 76-90 minutes.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Standout Performances
sections.text: Mallorca’s Leo Román has been in exceptional form recently, averaging a Sofascore rating of 9.4 over the last 5 matches as a goalkeeper, which could be pivotal in keeping Valladolid at bay. Real Valladolid’s Iván Sánchez has consistently performed against Mallorca, starting all 5 of their last encounters and averaging a 7.26 rating. Mallorca’s headers scored tally of 8 places them 6th in the league, potentially exploiting Valladolid’s defensive frailties. Meanwhile, Mallorca’s rank of 1 for opponent’s big chances created at just 55 suggests they rarely give away high-quality opportunities, a stat that could frustrate Valladolid’s attack.
